column3: description
Years alone hone the edge. Your next EDGE check this turn is made with a steadier hand and a colder eye.
No lord's protocols cloud your sight. Study one enemy; you learn a weakness the balance agent will tune, and your next strike against them bites deeper.

column1: # === L3 Ascendant Blade (4) — precision, refusal, reforged discipline ===

column3: Take the stance no master taught. Until your next turn, the first attack against you is simply declined — blade-turned, footwork perfect, refused.
A strike refined by ten thousand solitary repetitions. Precision-weighted EDGE attack; treats armor as thinner than it is.
Breathe. Reset. The stance collects itself. Discard any one card from hand; draw one. If the discarded card was a combat_card, your next attack this turn gains advantage.
For one moment there is no court, no lord, no school — only the cut. EDGE attack that cannot miss a target you are alone with (no allies adjacent).

column1: # === L3 Iron Contract (4) — hired steel, marks, contract ethics ===

column3: Point at one enemy and speak the word. That target becomes your Mark until end of encounter; your attacks against Marks hit harder, and you refuse attacks against anyone else unless paid in kind.
The contract is specific. Spend 1 AP to deal an IRON-weighted strike to your Mark; if it hits, you recover AP spent on this card next turn.
When a Mark dies, the contract pays out. Draw two cards and regain 1 AP; the ledger is closed.
The contract forbids waste. When an enemy attacks an ally, make an EDGE reaction against that enemy; if you hit, the attack is redirected to you and a debt-token is placed on the attacker.

column1: # === L3 Returning Blade (4) — wanderer's blade that comes back, debts that follow ===

column3: Hurl the blade at range — it strikes, then returns to your hand next turn. If it kills the target, it returns this turn instead.
Mark an enemy with a debt they did not agree to. At the start of each of your turns while they live, deal minor IRON damage; the debt follows them even if they flee the scene.
You have walked this road before. Move and then make an EDGE strike; if your movement ended behind or flanking the target, the strike returns the card to your hand at end of turn.
A discarded card is never truly gone. Shuffle one Ronin card from your discard back into your deck; if you have no discard, draw a card instead.
Every scar is a lesson paid in blood. Count your missing health; convert a portion of it into a single devastating EDGE+IRON strike against one enemy. The more wounded you are, the worse the cut.
You decline the battle's terms. Until start of your next turn, you cannot be targeted by any enemy whose name you do not know; your first strike after this ends becomes a critical.
Your broken resonance signature becomes the battle's rhythm. Make three EDGE strikes against up to three different enemies; each hit inflicts a dim flicker on their next action.

The cut that every empty-handed kata was preparing you for. Declare one enemy; make a single EDGE attack that bypasses armor and resonance-shielding. If the target survives, they cannot attack you until your next turn — they cannot explain what just happened.
Adopt the stance that has no lineage. For the rest of the encounter, each time you decline an attack (via Refusal Stance or reaction), you may immediately make a free Honed Cut against the attacker.
You draft the terms mid-combat. Choose any two enemies as Marks simultaneously; while both live, your strikes against them cost 1 less AP (minimum 1). When either dies, draw two cards.

The contract ends the way contracts always end — with someone collecting. Make an IRON-weighted attack against your Mark with advantage; if it kills them, every debt-token in play detonates, damaging their bearers.
You let the sword leave your hand, and the world brings it back. Make a ranged EDGE attack against every enemy in a line; the card returns to your hand at end of turn regardless of outcome.

Every long debt, every old road, every wound you left unanswered arrives in the same moment. All enemies you have marked, debted, or wounded this encounter take immediate IRON damage; shuffle your discard back into your deck.
